SurvivalGuide_walkingrunning_description: To move your virtual legs, use %1%2%3%4. This is called walking. <LINE> You can also tap %5 to Run, or %6 to Sprint. <LINE> Careful now - Running or Sprinting is loud and can attract the dead.
To move your virtual legs, use %1. This is called walking. <LINE> You can also tap %2 to Run, or %3 to Sprint. <LINE> Careful now - Running or Sprinting is loud and can attract the dead.
SurvivalGuide_climbing_description: You can climb over tall fences by tapping %1 when standing next to them. <LINE> Watch your Encumbrance - carrying a heavy load can make it difficult to climb!
SurvivalGuide_vaulting_description: You can hop fences by tapping %1. You will also vault them automatically when running - but that doesn't work so well with walls. <LINE> Mind your step - clumsy folk may trip and fall!
SurvivalGuide_aiming_description: Hold down %1 and move your mouse to look around and aim. <LINE> Remember to check behind you - though there's probably nothing there!
SurvivalGuide_strafing_description: Move while aiming to Strafe. <LINE> Strafing is a way to sneak while looking around, though you will move at a slower pace.
SurvivalGuide_sneak_fence_description: Press %1 to sneak and keep low. To hide more effectively, sneak beside short fences and vehicles. <LINE> Crouching will not stop the undead from seeing or hearing you if you get too close!  <LINE> Be mindful it uses more Endurance than simply walking.
SurvivalGuide_backpacks_description: You can drag and drop items from your inventory into backpacks, containers, and inventory slots. <LINE> The left inventory window shows everything you are carrying. The right window shows the contents of any nearby containers or items on the ground. <LINE> Use %1 to select multiple items, or %2 to select individual items.
SurvivalGuide_backpacks_description_joyp: You can drag and drop items from your inventory into backpacks, containers, and inventory slots. <LINE> The left inventory window shows everything you are carrying. The right window shows the contents of any nearby containers or items on the ground.
SurvivalGuide_double_click_description: Double click an item to do a contextual action. <LINE> It will equip clothes and weapons, read a book, or grab an item from a container.
SurvivalGuide_weight_distribution_descri: How much you can carry is limited by your Strength and Moodles. <LINE> Items in equipped bags weigh less than those in your main inventory. <LINE> Different bags have different capacities and weight reductions.
SurvivalGuide_right_click_interact_descr: Many items have advanced options which can be found using %1. <LINE> Try it on televisions, radios, generators, and even a toilet bowl if you're desperate!
SurvivalGuide_doors_description: Press %1 or %2 to open and close doors. Doors keep zombies out, and can be barricaded for extra strength. <LINE> Just don't depend on it holding forever!
SurvivalGuide_doors_description_joypad: Press %1 to open and close doors. Doors keep zombies out, and can be barricaded for extra strength. <LINE> Just don't depend on it holding forever!
SurvivalGuide_windows_description: Tap %1 once beside a window to open or close it. Hold %2 to climb through. <LINE> You can force a locked window open quicker by tapping %1, but sometimes its lock will break. You can still smash it open - but be mindful of broken glass!
SurvivalGuide_curtains_description: Curtains and sheets can be attached to windows to block their view. <LINE> Open and close them using %1 and %2.
SurvivalGuide_curtains_description_joypa: Curtains and sheets can be attached to windows to block their view. <LINE> Open and close them using %1 and select "Open Curtains".
SurvivalGuide_lights_description: %1 powered lights or light switches, to turn them on and off. <LINE> Remember, more light means it's easier for the undead to see you too!
SurvivalGuide_map_description: Use %1 or click %2 on the left to open your map and see where you are. The map can be filled in through exploration and by finding Maps or Flyers to reveal locations. <LINE> You can make markings on your map using a pen or pencil. <LINE> Don't get lost!
SurvivalGuide_map_description_joypad: Use %1 and select %2 to open your map and see where you are.  The map can be filled in through exploration and by finding Maps or Flyers to reveal locations. <LINE> You can make markings on your map using a pen or pencil. <LINE> Don't get lost!
SurvivalGuide_flashlights_description: Equipped flashlights can be turned on or off with %1. <LINE> All flashlights need battery power to work, and make you more visible in the dark. <LINE> Flashlights don't scare away the monsters.
SurvivalGuide_television_description: Televisions and radios let you tune into news, music, and more. Some let you watch VHS tapes or CDs. <LINE> Certain shows will give you skill points or new recipes. <LINE> Just don't fall in love with the Woodcraft guy.
SurvivalGuide_bad_gases_description: With appropriate skills and recipes, you can connect a generator to your house to provide power. <LINE> Never use a generator indoors - the fumes will damage you!
SurvivalGuide_equip_primary_description: Equip a weapon by using %1 on it, then selecting "Equip Primary". <LINE> You can use anything from a katana to a keytar - though results may differ!
SurvivalGuide_melee_attack_description: Aim an equipped melee weapon by holding %1, and use %2 to strike. <LINE> You strike towards the isometric cursor. <LINE> If a zombie is too close for a weapon's range, you will shove them instead.
SurvivalGuide_shove_description: Press %1 to shove a zombie away. You can also press %2 to stomp a downed zombie. <LINE> Using your feet will spare your weapon's condition. <LINE> Make sure to aim for the head!
SurvivalGuide_stealth_kill_description: Feeling brave? Equip a knife and sneak close behind an enemy. Aim, wait for your hand to raise - then strike! Silent but deadly.
SurvivalGuide_shooting_description: Aim an equipped gun with %1, and use %2 to fire. The smaller the crosshair, the more accurate your shot will be. <LINE> Your chance of hitting anything is influenced by your Skills, Moodles, light levels, and more. <LINE> Try not to shoot yourself in the foot.
SurvivalGuide_reload_description: Use %1 to reload using unboxed ammo. Ammo can be unboxed using the %2 menu. Some guns require Magazines to work. Hold %3 for more advanced reloading options.
SurvivalGuide_zombie_attacks_description: If the undead surround you, they can drag you down and kill you instantly! <LINE> Attacks from the rear are especially dangerous. Sadly, there is no way to make friends with a zombie.
SurvivalGuide_shouting_description: Press %1 to shout and attract nearby zombies. Do this while crouching and you will whisper. <LINE> You can use some items to make louder noises like whistles, harmonicas and more. <LINE> Despite reports to the contrary, shouting, unfortunately, does not cure the Knox Virus.
SurvivalGuide_shouting_description_joypa: Press %1 and select %2 to shout and attract nearby zombies. Do this while crouching and you will whisper. <LINE> You can use some items to make louder noises like whistles, harmonicas and more. <LINE> Despite reports to the contrary, shouting, unfortunately, does not cure the Knox Virus.
SurvivalGuide_hotbar_description: Use the hotbar for quicker access to your weapons. Use %1 to attach a weapon to a hotbar slot, then press the corresponding number or %2 to equip it. <LINE> Worn belts and holsters give you more hotbar slots to use.
SurvivalGuide_hotbar_description_joypad: Use the hotbar for quicker access to your weapons. Use %1 to attach a weapon to a hotbar slot, then press %2 to equip it. <LINE> Worn belts and holsters give you more hotbar slots to use.
SurvivalGuide_fence_defense_description: Zombies will stumble when climbing over fences or through windows <LINE> You can use this to your advantage - if they don't lunge and get you first!
SurvivalGuide_open_cans_description: Food in cans or jars can be opened using the Crafting menu or the %1 menu on it. <LINE> Cans need a Can Opener or a Blade to open.
SurvivalGuide_food_preparation_descripti: Use the Crafting menu or %1 on cooking containers like Bowls, Roasting Pans, or Saucepans to prepare food recipes. <LINE> Some may need the container to be filled with water first. <LINE> You can learn new recipes from magazines and TV shows.
SurvivalGuide_cooking_description: Put your food item into an oven, microwave, BBQ or other appliances, then turn it on using the button on the bottom to start cooking your food. <LINE> Don't forget to turn your oven off - unless you have a fire extinguisher to hand!
SurvivalGuide_cooking_description_joypad: Put your food item into an oven, microwave, BBQ or other appliances, then turn it by using %1 to start cooking your food. <LINE> Don't forget to turn your oven off - unless you have a fire extinguisher to hand!
SurvivalGuide_liquid_description: Use %1 to fill a Liquid Container eg. a Water Bottle, and see its options. <LINE> Use the Transfer Liquid menu for more advanced options. <LINE> Open topped containers can collect rainwater. Note that Tainted water is bad for your tummy! %2
SurvivalGuide_treat_water_description: Tainted water (usually from a river or collected rain) is unsafe for drinking. <LINE> Boil or use Purification Tablets to make it safe.
SurvivalGuide_moodles_description: The Moodles on the right represent your character's needs. <LINE> Green is good %1, red is bad %2. <LINE> If you see this one, %3, you don't need to worry about anything anymore.
SurvivalGuide_eating_drinking_descriptio: Double click or use %1 on a food or drink item to consume it. <LINE> By default, clean water in your main inventory will be automatically consumed to keep your thirst at bay. <LINE> Remember to eat a balanced diet or you may gain or lose weight!

Use %1 on a food or drink item to consume it. <LINE> By default, clean water in your main inventory will be automatically consumed to keep your thirst at bay. <LINE> Remember to eat a balanced diet or you may gain or lose weight!
SurvivalGuide_first_aid_description: Use %1 or click %2 on the left to open your health menu, and %3 to apply remedies to your injuries %4. <LINE> Many items of clothing can be ripped to get a Rag to use as a bandage. <LINE> To avoid infections use a disinfectant on the bandage or directly on your wound.
SurvivalGuide_first_aid_description_joyp: Use %1 to open your health menu %2, navigate the tabs using %3/%4, and %5 to apply remedies to your injuries %6. <LINE> Many items of clothing can be ripped to get a Rag to use as a bandage. <LINE> To avoid infections use a disinfectant on the bandage or directly on your wound.
SurvivalGuide_rest_description: Don't let tiredness %1 and exhaustion %2 sneak up on you! It will severely limit your awareness and capabilities. <LINE> Rest in a chair to catch your breath, and sleep in a comfy bed for better results. <LINE> Sleeping Tablets or Painkillers may help - but best not to mix them with alcohol.
SurvivalGuide_exercise_description: Running or moving heavy loads will make you stronger overtime. <LINE> But you can also gain Strength and Fitness using exercises on the Health menu. Be mindful of muscle strains or the next day you'll be in dire pain. <LINE> Even in the apocalypse, you can be beach body ready!
SurvivalGuide_skill_books_description: Some books will give you a Skill Multiplier to level up a skill faster. <LINE> You can also learn Crafting Recipes from Magazines, VHS tapes, or some other special items. <LINE> Leveling up skills will sometimes unlock new Recipes too.
SurvivalGuide_bad_smells_description: The apocalypse is filled with nasty smells. %1 <LINE> Don't hang around in areas with lots of dead bodies for too long, or you may get sick. Move away, bury or burn them, or wear a mask!
SurvivalGuide_crafting_menu_description: Press %1 or click %2 on the left to open the Crafting menu. <LINE> Use %3-%4 on an item to search how to produce it, and on an output item to search where it can be used.
SurvivalGuide_crafting_menu_description_: Press %1 and select %2 to open the Crafting menu.
SurvivalGuide_crafting_inventory_descrip: Some Crafting Recipes can be created almost anywhere using the Crafting Menu, with the proper materials and tools. Some simple Recipes can be crafted directly from your inventory using %1.
SurvivalGuide_craft_on_surface_descripti: Some Crafting Recipes need a hard surface like a table or a workstation - or even a boulder! - to be performed. <LINE> Stand beside one of these surfaces and open the Crafting menu %1 to see them.
SurvivalGuide_sheet_ropes_description: Use %1 on a window to attach an Escape Rope with a nail. You need just a single nail and a hammer, but one rope for every floor to the ground (eg. two ropes from the second floor). <LINE> We haven't taught the zombies how to climb (yet), but they can still tear down your rope.
SurvivalGuide_build_menu_description: Click %1 on the left to access the Build Menu. <LINE> This allows you, with the correct materials, to build static world objects and furniture like Fences, Walls, or Workstations. <LINE> Go and build yourself a post-apocalyptic mansion!
SurvivalGuide_build_menu_description_joy: Press %1 and select %2 to access the Build Menu. This allows you, with the correct materials, to build static world objects like Fences, Walls, or Workstations. <LINE> Go and build yourself a post-apocalyptic mansion!
SurvivalGuide_barricades_description: Use the Build Menu %1 or use %2 on a window or door to Barricade it. You'll need a Plank, Nails, and a Hammer for a basic wood Barricade. <LINE> Barricading gives door and windows more Health.
SurvivalGuide_build_walls_description: Wood or Metal walls require a Wallframe to be built first. <LINE> You can then fill the frame with walls, door frames or window frames. <LINE> A door will first need a door frame, while a window will need...  you guessed it, a window frame!
SurvivalGuide_crafting_station_descripti: Building workstations will let you create more advanced recipes. <LINE> There are many to build, from Furnaces to Churn Buckets and Drying Racks, some with unique functions!
SurvivalGuide_start_vehicle_description: If you're in a vehicle and have its key, use %1 to start the engine. Use %2%3%4%5 to drive and steer. Drive cautiously! Engine noise will attract zombies. <LINE> A vehicle's dashboard will show you important information, and allow you to unlock the doors or trunk.
SurvivalGuide_start_vehicle_description_: If you're in a vehicle and have its key, use %1 to start the engine. Use %2 to drive and steer. Drive cautiously! Engine noise will attract zombies. <LINE> A vehicle's dashboard will show you important information.
SurvivalGuide_vehicle_radial_menu_descri: Press %1 in or near a vehicle to see its options. <LINE> This will allow you to do things like add Gas, lock doors, or smash the car's windows.
SurvivalGuide_gas_refill_description: Without Gas, a vehicle can't move! Use the %1 menu to add Gas, if you have a container with Gas in your inventory or are beside a gas pump. <LINE> You can fill any Liquid Container with Gas from a powered pump. <LINE> Remember to check your fuel gauge to see which side the gas cap is on.
SurvivalGuide_mechanics_menu_description: Press %1 by your vehicle's hood, or use the %2 menu and select %3, to see your vehicle's condition. A vehicle in poor condition may need repairs. <LINE> The Mechanic skill makes this easier! And no nasty repair bills afterwards.
SurvivalGuide_trailers_description: Use the %1 menu to attach %2 or detach trailers %3 close to the back of your vehicle. <LINE> Trailers add extra storage space or allow you to transport animals, but also affect the vehicle's handling.
SurvivalGuide_vehicle_storage_descriptio: Vehicle trunks can store a large amount of items - or a corpse or two. <LINE> Glove compartments and even seats can also be used as storage space.
SurvivalGuide_seasons_and_weather_descri: It's not just zombies that are a threat in Knox Country. <LINE> Rain, snow, fog, and other adverse weather conditions can freeze or wet you, and lower visibility, especially in winter. Don't catch cold!
SurvivalGuide_temperature_description: Appropriate clothing will keep your temperature regulated. Too high or low temperatures will negatively affect you. <LINE> Some clothing offers protection from more than just the elements, and can even be reinforced with Tailoring!
SurvivalGuide_foraging_description: Click the spyglass icon %1 on the left to open the Investigate Area menu. <LINE> This window shows details on your surroundings. Click "Enable Search Mode" to begin Foraging. You may find Berries, Mushrooms, Animal Tracks - and lots more. <LINE> The menu will even show you the position of the sun, and phase of the moon!
SurvivalGuide_foraging_description_joypa: Pressing %1 and selecting the spyglass icon %2 to open the Investigate Area menu. <LINE> This window shows details on your surroundings. Press %3 to begin Foraging. You may find Berries, Mushrooms, Animal Tracks - and lots more. <LINE>  The menu will even show you the position of the sun, and phase of the moon!
SurvivalGuide_mining_description: You can use a pickaxe to remove small-to-medium rocks or other obstacles in the world. <LINE> Aim at the rock and press %1 to break it down into usable pieces. <LINE> Do not hit your pet rock by accident.
SurvivalGuide_open_seeds_description: Seeds have to be removed from their Packets before planting. <LINE> You can do so by using %1 on a seed packet, or using the Crafting menu.
SurvivalGuide_dig_furrow_description: Seeds can only be planted in outdoor furrows, at ground level. Use a Trowel, Shovel or, if you're desperate, your hands to dig a furrow in soil or grass. <LINE> To do so use %1 and select Dig Furrows under Gardening. <LINE> You might also find some wiggly worms - and you can call them Jim if you want to!
SurvivalGuide_sow_seeds_description: Plant seeds by using %1 on a dug furrow. <LINE> You can then use %1 or %2 on your plant to check its information panel. <LINE> Be mindful of the season and needs of your crops.
SurvivalGuide_sow_seeds_description_joyp: Plant seeds by using %1 on a dug furrow. <LINE> You can then use %1 on your plant to check its information panel. <LINE> Be mindful of the season and needs of your crops.
SurvivalGuide_harvesting_description: Done all the hard work? It's time to harvest your crops! Use the "Harvest option" by pressing %1 on the plant. <LINE> A high Agriculture skill means more crops will be harvested.
SurvivalGuide_animal_zone_description: Click %1 on the left to create Zones for your animals. <LINE> Calm animals will never leave their Zone, even if it's unfenced, as long as they have water and no zombies are around... <LINE> A Zone's info panel shows its food, water, and all animals within the zone.
SurvivalGuide_animal_zone_description_jo: Press %1 and select %2 to create Zones for your animals. <LINE> Calm animals will never leave their Zone, even if it's unfenced, as long as they have water and no zombies are around... <LINE>  A Zone's info panel shows its food, water, and all animals within the zone.
SurvivalGuide_animal_menu_description: Press %1 near an animal or %2 on it to find options for your it, including their Info. <LINE> Skilled Animal Carers will see more information on their animals.
SurvivalGuide_animal_upkeep_description: Animals need food and water to survive, which can be added to troughs or dropped in their Zone. <LINE> Animals will breed on their own. Just make sure your animals' bloodlines remain a tree, and not a circle!
SurvivalGuide_animal_stress_description: Being around zombies, noise, or shooting nearby will create Stress for animals, and affect their health. <LINE> Building trust is important with your animals. Time, love, and plenty of treats will help. <LINE> Don't stress the bull or you'll get the horns!
SurvivalGuide_animal_rope_description: You can put a rope on an animal by pressing %1 near it to move them around easily. <LINE> You can also attach them to a tree or a pole. <LINE> Be aware they can break the rope if too stressed.
SurvivalGuide_animal_hutch_description: Chickens and turkeys need a hutch to sleep safely at night. You can use %1 to open the hutch menu and watch over them. <LINE> Be sure to close the door at night, and open it in the morning to not stress your birds. <LINE> You must have a rooster with your hen to fertilize eggs.
SurvivalGuide_butchering_description: Animals can be killed by combat or by using the %1 menu near them. <LINE> While big animals require a Knife, smaller ones can be killed by hand! Just like Grandma used to do. <LINE> Animals butchered on a Butcher Hook will provide Leather and more Meat.
SurvivalGuide_petting_description: Petting an animal will reduce its Stress - and yours! The Stress reduction is limited to once per day. <LINE> You can even befriend rats. The apocalypse is a lonely place after all!
SurvivalGuide_fishing_zone_description: Ripples indicate fishable water. <LINE> Use %1 to open the Fishing Panel. This shows fish you've caught, along with information affecting the fish. <LINE> Skilled Fishers see more information.
SurvivalGuide_add_bait_description: Add bait to your Fishing Rod using %1 on it to increase your chance of catching a fish. <LINE> Different bait will attract different types of fish.
SurvivalGuide_cast_and_catch_description: With your Rod equipped, use %1 and %2 to cast. <LINE> Watch the tension meter to see when you have a bite, then reel in with %3. <LINE> Reeling too fast will snap your line.

With your Rod equipped, use %1 and %2 to cast. <LINE> Watch the tension meter to see when you have a bite, turn counter-clockwise %3 to reel in and clockwise to release tension. <LINE> Reeling too fast will snap your line.
SurvivalGuide_trapping_description: Place Traps in the world to catch small animals. Use %1 on a Trap in your inventory to place it, then use the %2 menu on it to add Bait. <LINE> Baited Traps only catch animals if you're far enough away for some time. <LINE> Traps can catch living animals like Rabbits or Raccoons, but some animals, like Birds, will always be killed by Traps.
SurvivalGuide_burn_corpse_description: Too many corpses in one area causes Sickness. Hold %1 beside a corpse to drag it. <LINE> Corpses can be buried in graves or burned with gasoline and a fire source. <LINE> The latter is better for your back!
SurvivalGuide_cleaning_area_description: The apocalypse can be a messy place. Use %1 with a cleaning fluid (eg. Bleach, Gasoline) and a cleaning tool (eg. a Sponge) to see cleaning options.
SurvivalGuide_clean_self_description: Keep your face and body clean with Water. A dirty body makes wound infections more likely! Soap will do a better job. <LINE> Seriously, wash yourself once in a while, we can smell you through the screen!
SurvivalGuide_laundry_description: Eventually, your clothes will be covered with dirt and/or blood. These make wound infections (but not zombie infection) more likely, so keep them clean with Soap and Water. <LINE> You can even use a washing machine if you're too lazy to do it by hand.
SurvivalGuide_activate_pvp_description: Click on %1 to turn PvP Safety on or off. <LINE> When PvP Safety is off, a skull will appear beside your username. <LINE> Two players with PvP Safety on cannot attack each other, but a player with PvP Safety off can attack anyone.
SurvivalGuide_faction_menu_description: Click on %1, then Create Faction to create an invite-only group, with private chat. <LINE> PvP can be disabled against fellow members, and members show on each other's Maps. <LINE> Note: faction members don't necessarily share Safehouses, which is done using the Safehouse menu.
SurvivalGuide_multiplayer_chat_descripti: Press %1 to type in chat. Your chat can be preceded by commands: /all (global chat, won't attract zombies), /say or /s (local chat), /whisper or /w followed by username (private message), <LINE> /yell or /y (shout), /safehouse or /sh (safehouse members), /faction or /f (faction members), <LINE> /admin or /a (admin chat), /r or /radio (message using equipped radio).
SurvivalGuide_medical_check_description: Use %1 on a player and select the option to perform a Medical Check. <LINE> Here you can provide aid to other players - or check for a dreaded bite mark.
